title: ELC(ℝ) Classification of Probability Distribution PDFs

framework.ELC_definition: exp-log closure of rationals over ℝ
framework.inside_ELC: functions expressible using only: +, -, *, /, exp, log, rational constants, integer powers
framework.outside_ELC: functions requiring Gamma (non-elementary), sin/cos (infinitely many zeros, oscillatory), or other transcendentals

distributions.name: Normal
distributions.classification: INSIDE ELC (x > 0 domain); DOMAIN FLAG (all ℝ)
distributions.domain_note: x² = mul(x,x) requires one mul. For x < 0 no new non-ELC function is added (x² is symmetric), but the domain convention for half-normal contexts from X7/X8 applies: ELC is cleanest on x > 0. Full real line: still ELC, just note symmetry.

operator_breakdown.sqrt_2pi: constant (pre-computed rational approximation or exact ELC expression sqrt = pow(,1/2))

distributions.notes: √(2π) = exp(½·log(2π)) — itself ELC. No Γ involved. Connects to X7/X8: real-line domain analysis.

distributions.name: Exponential
distributions.notes: Purely exponential family member. Minimal cost. Canonical ELC distribution.

distributions.name: Log-normal
distributions.notes: All operations are log, pow, exp, mul, div — pure ELC. x > 0 domain is natural. No Γ.

distributions.name: Poisson
distributions.pdf: P(k) = e^(−λ) · λ^k / k!,  k ∈ ℕ₀
distributions.classification: INSIDE ELC (integer k); OUTSIDE ELC (continuous k via Γ(k+1))
distributions.domain_note: k must be a non-negative integer for ELC membership

operator_breakdown.k_factorial_integer: constant at each integer k (lookup / accumulation, not a function)

distributions.notes: Integer k: k! is a finite product of rationals — ELC. Continuous extension k → Γ(k+1) exits ELC. Structural bifurcation: discrete PMF is INSIDE, continuous interpolation OUTSIDE.

distributions.name: Chi-squared
distributions.outside_reason: Γ(k/2) in normalisation constant — Gamma function is non-elementary
distributions.notes: Even if k is an even integer (making Γ(k/2) a rational multiple of an integer factorial), the general parametric form requires Γ. For specific even k, becomes INSIDE at that fixed k.

distributions.name: Beta
distributions.outside_reason: B(α,β) = Γ(α)·Γ(β)/Γ(α+β) — normalisation requires Gamma function
distributions.notes: Numerator x^(α−1)·(1−x)^(β−1) is ELC (pow, sub, mul). Denominator Beta function exits ELC. For integer α, β the Beta function reduces to rationals — INSIDE at those fixed integer parameters.

distributions.name: Gamma distribution
distributions.outside_reason: Γ(α) in normalisation — directly the Gamma function
distributions.notes: Archetype of Γ-dependent distributions. For integer α (Erlang distribution), Γ(α) = (α−1)! is rational → INSIDE at those parameters.

distributions.name: Weibull
distributions.notes: Pure exponential-family structure. pow(x/λ, k) via exp(k·log(x/λ)) = 3 ops each. No Γ anywhere. Generalises exponential (k=1) and Rayleigh (k=2).

distributions.name: Pareto
distributions.pdf: f(x) = α · x_m^α / x^(α+1),  x ≥ x_m
distributions.notes: Power-law distribution. Both pow operations via exp-log. All operations elementary. No Γ.

distributions.name: Cauchy
distributions.notes: π appears as a constant (ELC: π = 4·arctan(1), but can also be treated as a known constant ∈ closure). No Γ, no oscillatory functions. Clean ELC despite heavy tails. Normalisation uses π directly — not Γ.

CONDITIONAL_INSIDE: Gamma distribution at integer α (= Erlang)

key_finding.structural_split: The ELC boundary in statistics separates the exponential family from the Γ-dependent distributions.
key_finding.exponential_family_inside: Normal, Exponential, Log-normal, Weibull, Pareto, Cauchy — all have PDFs expressible purely in ELC(ℝ).
key_finding.gamma_dependent_outside: Chi-squared, Beta, and Gamma distribution all require Γ in their normalisation constant, placing them outside ELC(ℝ).
key_finding.mechanism: Γ arises as the normalisation constant for distributions whose support or shape parameters generalise beyond integer values. Where integer constraints hold, Γ(n) = (n−1)! reduces to a rational, pulling the distribution back inside ELC.
key_finding.discrete_vs_continuous: Poisson is an edge case: its PMF is INSIDE ELC for integer k (k! is rational), but the continuous interpolation via Γ(k+1) exits ELC.
key_finding.pi_vs_gamma: Cauchy uses π as a normalisation constant — π is treatable as an ELC constant. This contrasts with Γ, which is a genuinely non-elementary function. π ∈ ELC; Γ ∉ ELC.
key_finding.connection_to_prior_sessions: X7/X8 established domain boundary behaviour for ELC functions on ℝ. The Normal distribution's full-line domain is consistent with ELC — the split was a pedagogical note, not a true exclusion.
key_finding.elc_boundary_conjecture: A distribution's PDF is in ELC(ℝ) if and only if its normalisation constant can be expressed without the Gamma function (or equivalently: without Γ evaluated at non-integer arguments).

pow_operator_cost_note: pow(a, b) = exp(b · log(a)): costs exp(1) + log(1) + mul(1) = 3n when b is a non-rational real parameter. When b is a fixed rational, pow can be cheaper (e.g., pow(x,2) = mul(x,x) = 1n).
